# Generated Output

Running `calimero-abi-codegen -i abi.json -o src/generated --client-name KvStoreClient` produces a
**single file** named after the client class — types are embedded inline, there is no separate
`types.ts`:

```text
src/generated/
└── KvStoreClient.ts
```

## KvStoreClient.ts

One file containing: a `MeroJs` import, all TypeScript types matching the ABI types, event payload
types, the `AbiEvent` union, an optional `CalimeroBytes` helper (only when a type uses bytes), and
the typed client class with one method per ABI function:

```typescript
/** @generated by @calimero/abi-codegen — DO NOT EDIT. */

import {
  MeroJs,
} from '@calimero-network/mero-react';

// Generated types

export interface Entry {
  key: string;
  value: string;
}

// Event payload interfaces (one per event with a struct payload)
export interface Event_ItemSet {
  key: string;
  value: string;
}

// AbiEvent union — discriminated on `name`; events with no data omit `payload`
export type AbiEvent =
  | { name: "ItemSet"; payload: Event_ItemSet }
  | { name: "ItemRemoved" }
;


export class KvStoreClient {
  private _mero: MeroJs;
  private _contextId: string;
  private _executorPublicKey: string;

  constructor(mero: MeroJs, contextId: string, executorPublicKey: string) {
    this._mero = mero;
    this._contextId = contextId;
    this._executorPublicKey = executorPublicKey;
  }

  /**
   * set
   */
  public async set(params: { key: string; value: string }): Promise<void> {
    const response = await this._mero.rpc.execute({ contextId: this._contextId, method: 'set', argsJson: params, executorPublicKey: this._executorPublicKey });
    return response as void;
  }

  /**
   * get
   */
  public async get(params: { key: string }): Promise<string | null> {
    const response = await this._mero.rpc.execute({ contextId: this._contextId, method: 'get', argsJson: params, executorPublicKey: this._executorPublicKey });
    return response as string | null;
  }

  /**
   * entries
   */
  public async entries(): Promise<Entry[]> {
    const response = await this._mero.rpc.execute({ contextId: this._contextId, method: 'entries', argsJson: {}, executorPublicKey: this._executorPublicKey });
    return response as Entry[];
  }

}
```

Notes on the shape:

- Methods are named in `camelCase` (`send_message` → `sendMessage`); the on-wire `method` string
  keeps the original ABI name.
- A method with parameters takes a single `params` object; a method with no parameters takes no
  arguments and passes `argsJson: {}`.
- Each method calls `this._mero.rpc.execute({ contextId, method, argsJson, executorPublicKey })`.
  There is no `mutate`/`query` split — `rpc.execute` is used for every method (mero-js has no
  separate read transport yet; a method's `intent`, if present, is emitted only as an `@intent`
  JSDoc tag).
- `rpc.execute` returns the result directly (or throws an `RpcError`) — there is no
  `{ output }` / `{ error }` wrapper to unwrap.

## Enum / variant types

Rust enums map to one of two shapes:

```typescript
// All-unit variants → serde serializes them as bare strings, so the generator
// emits a string-literal union:
export type Status = 'Active' | 'Archived';

// Variants with payloads → a discriminated union plus a factory object:
export type ActionPayload =
  | { name: 'Move'; payload: { x: number; y: number } }
  | { name: 'Reset' };

export const Action = {
  Move: (move: { x: number; y: number }): ActionPayload => ({ name: 'Move', payload: move }),
  Reset: (): ActionPayload => ({ name: 'Reset' }),
} as const;
```

## Using the generated client

```typescript
import { useMero } from '@calimero-network/mero-react';
import { KvStoreClient } from './generated/KvStoreClient';

function useKvStore(contextId: string, executorPublicKey: string) {
  const { mero } = useMero(); // mero is MeroJs | null until connected
  return useMemo(
    () => (mero ? new KvStoreClient(mero, contextId, executorPublicKey) : null),
    [mero, contextId, executorPublicKey],
  );
}

// Fully typed — IDE autocomplete works
const client = useKvStore(contextId, executorPublicKey);
await client?.set({ key: 'hello', value: 'world' });
const value = await client?.get({ key: 'hello' }); // string | null
const all = await client?.entries(); // Entry[]
```

## Regenerating after app changes

When you update your Rust app and the ABI changes, re-run codegen:

```bash
# After rebuilding your WASM app (which regenerates abi.json):
npx calimero-abi-codegen -i abi.json -o src/generated --client-name KvStoreClient
```

Add this to your build script so it stays in sync automatically:

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "codegen": "calimero-abi-codegen -i abi.json -o src/generated --client-name KvStoreClient",
    "build": "npm run codegen && tsc"
  }
}
```
